metadata.title: Z² Global Health Pipeline: Geometric Therapeutic Design for Highest-Burden Diseases
metadata.description:
Computational therapeutic pipeline targeting the four highest-burden disease categories (DALYs): neurodegeneration, antimicrobial resistance, autoimmune disease, and metastatic cancer.

The Z² Principle:

Z² = 32π/3 ≈ 33.51 predicts a natural protein length scale of r = 9.14 Å. This scale appears consistently in pathological targets:

  • Alzheimer's amyloid steric zipper: 9.5 Å (96.1% match)
  • MBL active site depth: 10.0 Å (90.6% match)
  • IL-6R interface span: ~6 × 9.14 Å
  • RGD integrin spacing: 7.5 Å × 1.22 ≈ 9.14 Å

Therapeutic Modules:

  • med_04: Alzheimer's - Beta-sheet breaker peptides (ZIM-ALZ-005: Ac-FPF-NH2)
  • med_05: AMR Superbugs - Knotted MBL inhibitors (ZIM-AMR-001: trefoil knot)
  • med_06: Autoimmune - Cytokine interface cappers (ZIM-AI-004: 4×Z² macrocycle)
  • med_07: Cancer Metastasis - Sustained RGD decoys (ZIM-MET-004: PEG40k, 168h t½)
  • med_01: Cystic Fibrosis - ΔF508 void-filling chaperones (ZIM-CF-004: RFFR)
  • med_02: Opioid Addiction - hERG-safe nAChR modulators (ZIM-ADD-003: RWWFWR)
  • med_03: PROTAC Linkers - Z²-optimal ternary complex linkers

Key Safety Features:

  • hERG exclusion: GUARANTEED by steric impossibility (14 Å >> 4 Å filter)
  • Cilengitide lessons applied: Extended half-life for sustained coverage
  • All designs include comprehensive safety analysis

Legal Disclaimer:

THEORETICAL RESEARCH ONLY. Not peer reviewed. Not medical advice. All outputs are computational predictions with no warranty of efficacy or safety. Any drug development must comply with IND, GLP, GMP, and clinical trial regulations.

License:

AGPL-3.0-or-later for code, OpenMTA for biological materials. All sequences published as prior art.
metadata.notes: This work targets the highest-burden diseases as measured by DALYs. All therapeutic designs are released as prior art under AGPL-3.0-or-later to ensure open access and prevent restrictive patenting. DISCLAIMER: Theoretical research only. Not peer reviewed. Not medical advice.
